title: WF-015 hardening (1/3) — permission drift scan -> findings -> incident
business_reason: The other half of WF-015. permission_drift_findings + a scan job stub existed but nothing computed drift. Detect staff who still hold a role that is no longer active (deprecated/disabled/archived) or unknown — privilege that should have been revoked — and open an incident on high risk.

design.scan: server/security/permissionDrift.ts: PURE scanPermissionDrift(staff, roles) — for each staff member's held roles (primary_role + secondary_roles), a role whose registry status != active (or that is missing) is a finding, carrying the role's risk_level. maxRisk() drives incident severity. Unit-tested.
design.route: POST /api/staff/security-audit/drift-scan: loads staff_profiles + portal_roles, runs the scan, UPSERTs each finding (deterministic finding_code per staff+role -> idempotent), and on a high/critical finding opens (or reuses) an unauthorized_access incident; audited.
design.ui: added a 'Run permission drift scan' panel to /staff/security-audit/integrity (now Audit Integrity & Drift) — result + opened incident.

param_14.question: How do we catch privilege that should have been revoked?
param_14.answer: A scan compares every staff member's held roles (primary + secondary) against the role registry. A role that is no longer active (deprecated/disabled/archived) or that doesn't exist is flagged as drift, carrying that role's risk level. Findings are recorded (idempotently), and a high/critical drift opens an unauthorized_access security incident for remediation.
param_14.answer_source: staff_profiles roles vs portal_roles.role_status, permission_drift_findings, WF-015
param_14.security_implications: surfaces ungoverned/leftover privilege (decommissioned or unknown roles still assigned); high/critical drift -> an incident; findings are idempotent (no duplicate noise); read + finding/incident write only.
param_14.approval_implications: remediation follows the permission_drift_findings lifecycle (open -> remediation_task_created -> resolved) + the incident lifecycle.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: auto-create a remediation task per finding; baseline-snapshot drift (granted-vs-approved) once user-level role grants are governed; scheduled sweep (item 3).
